content:
🌟 project_aura | C
🎯 Primary Use Case: To build a DIY, ESP32-S3 powered air quality monitoring station with a graphical user interface and smart home integration.
✨ Key Features: • Professional telemetry: PM0.5/PM1/PM2.5/PM4/PM10, CO, CO2, VOC, NOx, temperature, humidity, absolute humidity (AH), pressure, HCHO. • No soldering required: designed for easy assembly using Grove/QT connectors.
📖 Summary: Project Aura AQ is an open-source ESP32-S3 based air quality station featuring a touch-friendly LVGL UI, local web setup, and MQTT integration for Home Assistant. It provides comprehensive air quality telemetry and is designed for easy assembly by makers.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 rdn | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: A modern, retro-inspired terminal file manager for efficient file navigation and operations.
✨ Key Features: • Two-panel interface • DOS-accurate color theme • File operations (copy, move, delete, mkdir) • Built-in file viewer (text/hex) • Quick search
📖 Summary: rdn is a terminal-based file manager written in Rust, inspired by the classic Dos Navigator. It offers a two-panel interface, essential file operations, and a retro DOS-accurate color theme, aiming to bring a familiar orthodox file manager experience to modern terminals.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 ai-sdk-tools | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with a suite of tools and utilities to enhance the development, debugging, and deployment of AI applications built with the Vercel AI SDK.
✨ Key Features: • AI chat state management • Development tools for debugging AI applications • Stream structured, type-safe artifacts from AI tools • Multi-agent orchestration • Universal caching for AI SDK tools
📖 Summary: This TypeScript package provides essential utilities for building production-ready AI applications using the Vercel AI SDK. It offers solutions for state management, debugging, structured streaming, intelligent agents, caching, and persistent memory, aiming to streamline AI development.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 Nano-PDF | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Editing PDF documents, including text, images, and slide content, through intuitive natural language commands.
✨ Key Features: • Natural Language Editing • Add New Slides • Non-Destructive Editing (OCR Re-hydration) • Multi-page & Parallel Processing
📖 Summary: Nano PDF Editor is a Python-based command-line tool that allows users to edit PDF documents using natural language prompts. It leverages Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model to understand and execute edits, preserving the original text layer through OCR re-hydration.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 iothackbot | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Comprehensive security testing and vulnerability assessment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and embedded systems.
✨ Key Features: • Network Discovery & Reconnaissance (wsdiscovery, iotnet, netflows, nmap) • Device-Specific Testing (onvifscan) • Firmware & File Analysis (chipsec, ffind) • Android Analysis (apktool, jadx) • Hardware & Console Access (picocom, telnetshell)
📖 Summary: IoTHackBot is an open-source toolkit for IoT security testing, offering a suite of command-line tools and AI-assisted Claude Code skills for discovering vulnerabilities in devices, firmware, and applications. It facilitates network reconnaissance, device-specific testing, firmware analysis, and Android app examination.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
